content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON: normal volatility (Vol: 25.3%) with full participation (Breadth: 8/8 sectors positive). Market tone favors cyclicals and banks after a broad one-week rally.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Banking & Finance leading: AKBNK (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND, ISCTR (1W: +9.03% | 1M: +1.59%) ABOVE TREND, GARAN (1W: +8.32% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND. Technology & Telecom strong: TCELL (1W: +7.02% | 1M: +2.60%) ABOVE TREND, KONTR (1W: +8.78% | 1M: +1.50%) ABOVE TREND.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Energy & Chemicals is the softest among winners: TUPRS (1W: -0.39% | 1M: +7.04%) CORRECTION, PETKM (1W: +X% | 1M: +X%) ABOVE TREND — monitor commodity sensitivity. Retail & Food trails relatively: ULKER (1W: -1.42% | 1M: -1.67%) BELOW TREND, BIMAS (1W: +5.05% | 1M: +13.26%) ABOVE TREND shows divergence within the sector.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
KRDMD (1W: +15.78% | 1M: +16.67%) ABOVE TREND; ASELS (1W: +11.96% | 1M: +12.72%) ABOVE TREND; ISCTR (1W: +9.03% | 1M: +1.59%) ABOVE TREND; KONTR (1W: +8.78% | 1M: +1.50%) ABOVE TREND; AKBNK (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
VESTL (1W: -3.57% | 1M: -3.71%) BELOW TREND; TRALT (1W: -3.10% | 1M: -21.05%) BELOW TREND; ULKER (1W: -1.42% | 1M: -1.67%) BELOW TREND; TUPRS (1W: -0.39% | 1M: +7.04%) CORRECTION; GUBRF (1W: +0.20% | 1M: -5.61%) RECOVERY.

6. Momentum Stocks?
KRDMD (1W: +15.78% | 1M: +16.67%) ABOVE TREND and ASELS (1W: +11.96% | 1M: +12.72%) ABOVE TREND are the clear momentum leaders; ISCTR (1W: +9.03% | 1M: +1.59%) ABOVE TREND, KONTR (1W: +8.78% | 1M: +1.50%) ABOVE TREND and AKBNK (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND round out the short-term movers.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banks are driving the rally; AKBNK (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND, GARAN (1W: +8.32% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND, ISCTR (1W: +9.03% | 1M: +1.59%) ABOVE TREND and YKBNK (1W: +8.72% | 1M: +1.73%) ABOVE TREND show momentum but watch earnings and NPL/cost of risk signals.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry strong: KRDMD (1W: +15.78% | 1M: +16.67%) ABOVE TREND, ASELS (1W: +11.96% | 1M: +12.72%) ABOVE TREND, EREGL (1W: +6.74% | 1M: +2.66%) ABOVE TREND; watch VESTL (1W: -3.57% | 1M: -3.71%) BELOW TREND for stock-specific weakness.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Mixed but defensive — SASA (1W: +0.41% | 1M: +8.41%) ABOVE TREND and SISE (1W: +3.89% | 1M: +10.10%) ABOVE TREND hold up, while TUPRS (1W: -0.39% | 1M: +7.04%) CORRECTION and TRALT (1W: -3.10% | 1M: -21.05%) BELOW TREND need commodity and refining margin checks.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Overweight banks and select cyclicals (heavy industry, transportation) given RISK-ON breadth; trim structurally weak or idiosyncratic laggards (TRALT, VESTL, ULKER). Keep some exposure to energy names with strong 1M momentum (SASA, SISE) but use stock-specific triggers for entries.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
KRDMD (1W: +15.78% | 1M: +16.67%) ABOVE TREND for momentum continuation; ASELS (1W: +11.96% | 1M: +12.72%) ABOVE TREND for defense/case wins; ISCTR (1W: +9.03% | 1M: +1.59%) ABOVE TREND and AKBNK (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +0.80%) ABOVE TREND for bank flow; TUPRS (1W: -0.39% | 1M: +7.04%) CORRECTION for a volatility trade.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Banking & Finance for flow and earnings sensitivity; Heavy Industry for momentum and export cyclicality; Energy & Chemicals for commodity-driven divergences.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 momentum — a decisive break above recent highs would confirm continuation; failure to hold the 20-day moving average (short-term pivot) would warn of mean reversion. Monitor Turkish macro headlines, CBRT guidance, bank earnings flow and commodity/refining margin data as primary catalysts.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Risk-on environment with normal volatility and perfect breadth; banks and heavy industry lead the move while energy shows mixed signals. Trade bias: overweight cyclicals and selective bank exposure, use stops on idiosyncratic laggards and watch macro/corporate catalysts for next-week direction.
